name: Jonathan
description: Jonathan, the eldest son of King Saul, is a prominent figure in the Hebrew Bible, particularly known for his unwavering loyalty and deep friendship with David. He was a skilled warrior and a prince of Israel, demonstrating courage and military prowess in battles against the Philistines. Despite being heir to the throne, Jonathan recognized David's divinely ordained destiny and selflessly supported David's claim to the kingship, even at the expense of his own ambition. His covenant of friendship with David is a cornerstone of their story, representing profound loyalty, mutual respect, and unwavering commitment. Jonathan's character embodies righteousness, humility, and a willingness to prioritize friendship and faith above personal gain. He represents a moral ideal of selfless friendship and devotion to a higher purpose. His death alongside his father at the Battle of Mount Gilboa is a tragic event that underscores the complexities of loyalty and destiny within the biblical narrative. Jonathan's actions serve as a powerful example of integrity and devotion, making him a revered figure in Jewish tradition. He chose friendship and righteousness over personal ambition, cementing his legacy as one of the most noble figures in the Tanakh. His story is a timeless lesson about the power of friendship and the importance of recognizing true leadership.
subtitle: Loyal Prince and Devoted Friend
achievements: Defeating the Philistine garrison at Geba
Forming a covenant of friendship with David

keyMyths.title: Jonathan's Attack on the Philistine Garrison
keyMyths.description: Jonathan bravely attacks and defeats a Philistine garrison at Geba, sparking a larger conflict between the Israelites and the Philistines. This act demonstrates his courage and initiative as a warrior. The story is found in 1 Samuel 13-14.

keyMyths.title: The Covenant of Friendship with David
keyMyths.description: Jonathan makes a covenant with David, symbolizing their deep friendship and mutual loyalty. He recognizes David's destiny and willingly supports him, despite his own claim to the throne. This is detailed in 1 Samuel 18.

keyMyths.title: Jonathan's Defense of David
keyMyths.description: Jonathan repeatedly defends David from his father Saul's jealousy and attempts to kill him. He risks his own life to warn David of Saul's intentions and protect him from harm. This is a recurring theme in 1 Samuel 19-23.

sources.author: The Hebrew Bible
sources.description: Primary source for the life and deeds of Jonathan.
sources.text: Primary source for the life and deeds of Jonathan.
